title: FAA looks to fine United Airlines $1.15 million for flying Boeing 777s that ‘were not in airworthy condition'
text: The Federal Aviation Administration proposed fining United Airlines Holdings Corp. $1.15 million for allegedly operating flights without performing proper maintenance inspections. The FAA said from June 2018 to April 2021, United Airlines conducted flights of Boeing 777 aircraft that “were not in airworthy condition,” because United allegedly removed in 2018 the Fire System Warning Check from its preflight check list.

title: FAA proposes more than $1 million fine on United Airlines over preflight safety checks
text: The FAA proposed a more than $1.1 million penalty against United Airlines over an alleged safety check issue.
